title: FAST View Template
description: A JSON schema describing the FAST declarative HTML template structure. Maps to the f-template declarative HTML syntax used in @microsoft/fast-html.

node.description: A template node — one of: static text, content binding, unescaped HTML, HTML element, when-directive, or repeat-directive.

textNode.description: A static text node with no bindings.

value.description: The static text content to render.

bindingNode.description: A content binding node. Equivalent to {{expression}} in declarative template syntax. Renders the resolved value as text content.

expression.description: A dot-notation property path to bind, e.g. 'greeting' or 'user.name'. Inside a repeat, prefix with the loop variable: 'item.name' for loop variable 'item'. Use '$c.parent.prop' to access the parent context from inside a repeat.

unescapedHtmlNode.description: An unescaped HTML binding node. Equivalent to {{{expression}}} in declarative template syntax. The resolved string value is injected as raw innerHTML inside a wrapper div.

expression.description: A property path whose value is rendered as unescaped HTML, e.g. 'htmlContent'.

elementNode.description: An HTML element node, including both standard and custom elements.

attributes.description: A map of attribute names to their binding configuration. For event attributes use the event name as key (e.g. 'click'). For property, boolean and bound attributes use the attribute/property name as key (e.g. 'value', 'disabled', 'title'). For directives use 'f-ref', 'f-slotted', or 'f-children' as the key.

children.description: Child template nodes nested inside this element.

whenNode.description: A conditional directive node. Equivalent to <f-when value="{{expression}}">...</f-when> in declarative template syntax.

expression.description: A boolean expression evaluated against the component source. Supports: simple property ('show'), negation ('!show'), comparisons ('count > 0', 'status == "active"', 'count != 0'), and logical chains ('show && active', 'a || b'). Use '$c.parent.prop' to access parent context inside a repeat.

children.description: Nodes to render when the expression is truthy.

repeatNode.description: A repeat directive node. Equivalent to <f-repeat value="{{item in items}}">...</f-repeat> in declarative template syntax.

item.description: The loop variable name used to reference the current item in child bindings, e.g. 'item'.

list.description: A dot-notation property path to the array to iterate over, e.g. 'items' or 'data.users'.

children.description: Nodes rendered for each item. In child binding expressions prefix with the loop variable: 'item.name' for loop variable 'item'. To access a host property use '$c.parent.prop'.

attributeValue.description: An attribute value — one of: static string, regular attribute binding, property binding, boolean attribute binding, event binding, ref directive, slotted directive, or children directive.

staticAttributeValue.description: A static (non-bound) attribute value. Rendered as a literal string attribute.

boundAttributeValue.description: A regular HTML attribute binding. Equivalent to attr="{{expression}}" in declarative template syntax. Use the plain attribute name as the key, e.g. 'title', 'aria-label'.

expression.description: A dot-notation property path bound to the attribute value.

propertyAttributeValue.description: A DOM property binding. Equivalent to :prop="{{expression}}" in declarative template syntax. Use the property name (without ':' prefix) as the key, e.g. 'value', 'innerHTML'.

expression.description: A dot-notation property path bound to the DOM property.

booleanAttributeValue.description: A boolean attribute binding. Equivalent to ?attr="{{expression}}" in declarative template syntax. Use the attribute name (without '?' prefix) as the key, e.g. 'disabled', 'checked', 'hidden'.

expression.description: A boolean expression. Supports the same syntax as whenNode.expression.

eventAttributeValue.description: An event listener binding. Equivalent to @event="{handler(arg)}" in declarative template syntax. Use the event name (without '@' prefix) as the key, e.g. 'click', 'keyup', 'input'.

handler.description: The method name on the component source to invoke when the event fires, e.g. 'handleClick'. Use '$c.parent.method' to invoke a method on a parent context (inside a repeat).

argument.description: Optional argument to pass to the handler. Use empty string (default) for no argument, 'e' to pass the DOM event object, or a property name to pass that property's value.

refDirectiveValue.description: A ref attribute directive. Equivalent to f-ref="{property}" in declarative template syntax. Assigns a reference to the DOM element onto the named component property. Use 'f-ref' as the attribute key.

property.description: The component property name to assign the element reference to, e.g. 'heading'.

slottedDirectiveValue.description: A slotted nodes directive. Equivalent to f-slotted="{property}" or f-slotted="{property filter elements(...)}" in declarative template syntax. Observes slotted child nodes and keeps the named property up to date. Use 'f-slotted' as the attribute key.

property.description: The component property name to assign the observed slotted nodes to.

filter.description: Optional filter applied to slotted nodes. Omit to include all nodes.

selectors.description: Optional CSS selectors to further restrict which elements are observed. If omitted or empty, all element nodes are included.

childrenDirectiveValue.description: A children nodes directive. Equivalent to f-children="{property}" in declarative template syntax. Observes child nodes and keeps the named property up to date. Use 'f-children' as the attribute key.

property.description: The component property name to assign the observed child nodes to.
